# ESA Incentive Pipeline — AI Agent Context

## What this system is

The ESA Incentive Pipeline is the internal tooling for **ESA (European Speedrunner Assembly)** marathons.
It ingests the accepted run schedule from Oengus and Horaro, manages donation incentives, and provides
host-facing briefs and runner data for use during the live event.

This API is the backend for the web viewer used by ESA hosts and admins during the marathon.

**Production base URL**: `https://esa-hosting.codescales.xyz`
**OpenAPI spec**: `https://esa-hosting.codescales.xyz/openapi.yaml`
**Interactive docs**: `https://esa-hosting.codescales.xyz/docs` (FastAPI Swagger UI)

---

## Authentication

Two cookie-based sessions. Both are set via POST — no Bearer tokens.

| Session cookie     | Set via                  | Lifetime | Scope                        |
|--------------------|--------------------------|----------|------------------------------|
| `esa_session`      | `POST /api/login`        | 24 h     | Read data, write notes       |
| `esa_admin_session`| `POST /api/admin/login`  | 1 h      | Admin sync, DB management    |

Passwords are shared secrets configured via environment variables (`SHARED_PASSWORD`, `ADMIN_PASSWORD`).
Pass `credentials: "include"` (or equivalent) on all requests.

---

## Core domain concepts

### Run
A scheduled speedrun at the marathon. Key fields:
- `slug` — URL-safe identifier, e.g. `dragon-ball-z-budokai-3__goku-s-story-z3-breakthrough__2026-08-01T1400`
- `scheduled` — ISO 8601 datetime (Europe/Stockholm timezone)
- `estimate` — HH:MM:SS string
- `stream` / `stream_short` — which stream this run is on (e.g. `stream1`)
- `participants[]` — array of runners (multi-runner races/co-op supported)

### Runner
A person running a game. Key fields:
- `slug` — derived from Twitch handle (lowercase) or `player-<display>-<id>` fallback
- `stats_json` — JSON blob from `runner-profile` CLI containing `summary` + `stats`

### Incentive
A donation incentive attached to a run. Categories: `Reward`, `Poll-Bid War`, `Target`.
Status lifecycle: `To-Do` → `In Review` → `Approved`.

### Brief
A host-facing document for each run. Two parts:
- `prose_md` — markdown text for the host
- `sidecar` — structured JSON with interview material, WR data, siblings, sources, confidence flags

---

## Key endpoints

### Schedule
```
GET /api/runs                          # All runs (optional: stream, window, next_hours, marathon=true)
GET /api/runs/{slug}                   # Single run
GET /api/streams                       # Available stream identifiers
```

### Runners
```
GET /api/runners/{slug}                # Identity + run count + upcoming runs
GET /api/runners/{slug}/profile        # Composite profile: summary stats + ESA history + tenure + PBs
GET /api/runners/{slug}/pbs            # Full speedrun.com personal bests table
GET /api/runners/{slug}/runs           # Upcoming runs for this runner
```

The `/profile` endpoint returns:
- `summary.event_count` — ESA events attended
- `summary.appearance_count` — total ESA run appearances
- `summary.first_event_year` — year of debut
- `summary.country_name` — self-reported, unverified
- `stats.past_esa.esa_runs[]` — per-game/category ESA history
- `stats.past_esa.events[]` — ordered list of event names
- `stats.past_esa.first_appearance` — first run details
- `stats.tenure.label` — e.g. "11 years on speedrun.com (since 2014-09-02)"
- `stats.communities[]` — ESA-adjacent communities found in SRC/Twitch bio

### Incentives
```
GET    /api/incentives                 # All incentives (optional: run_slug, status, category, stream)
GET    /api/incentives/{uuid}          # Single incentive
POST   /api/incentives                 # Create (host session required)
PATCH  /api/incentives/{uuid}          # Edit category/status/estimate (host session required)
DELETE /api/incentives/{uuid}          # Soft-delete (host session required)
```

### Briefs
```
GET /api/briefs/{slug}                 # Full brief for a run (prose_md + sidecar JSON)
GET /api/briefs                        # Brief index (optional: shift parameter)
```

### Notes
```
GET    /api/notes?run_slug={slug}      # Host notes for a run
POST   /api/notes                      # Create note
PATCH  /api/notes/{id}                 # Edit own note
DELETE /api/notes/{id}                 # Delete own note

GET    /api/runner-notes?runner_slug={slug}   # Per-runner notes
POST   /api/runner-notes
PATCH  /api/runner-notes/{id}
DELETE /api/runner-notes/{id}
```

### Admin (requires admin session)
```
GET  /api/admin/status                 # DB health, schema version, row counts
POST /api/admin/refresh                # Re-import xlsx → SQLite (async job)
POST /api/admin/sync/schedule          # Pull schedule from Horaro/Oengus
POST /api/admin/sync/briefs            # Regenerate host briefs
POST /api/admin/sync/runners           # Refresh runner profiles from SRC/lineup
GET  /api/admin/jobs                   # List background jobs
GET  /api/admin/jobs/{id}              # Poll job status (status: pending|running|completed|failed)
POST /api/admin/jobs/{id}/cancel       # Cancel a running job
GET  /api/admin/snapshots              # List DB snapshots
POST /api/admin/restore                # Restore from snapshot
GET  /api/admin/audit                  # Audit log (incentive/admin changes)
```

---

## Data sources and confidence

All runner profile data is sourced from:
1. **lineup.esamarathon.dev** — authoritative ESA attendance (event counts, country, first appearance)
2. **horaro.net** — fallback schedule scanning for older events
3. **speedrun.com** — PBs, tenure, community bio markers

Confidence levels on runner profiles:
- `high` — strong SRC match + lineup data
- `moderate` — partial match or display-name-only lookup
- `none` — lineup data only, no SRC account found

Claims marked `has_profile: false` have no verified data. Tenure marked "Not found — ask the runner"
when SRC lookup failed.

Country is always `(unverified)` — it is self-reported on the lineup form.

---

## Slug patterns

Run slugs follow the pattern: `{game-slug}__{category-slug}__{YYYY-MM-DDTHHMM}`

Runner slugs:
- Twitch handle (lowercase) if available — e.g. `tharixer`
- `player-{display-slug}-{run-id}` fallback for runners without Twitch

---

## Useful queries for AI agents

**Get the full marathon schedule:**
```
GET /api/runs?marathon=true
```

**Get a runner's complete ESA history:**
```
GET /api/runners/{slug}/profile
→ stats.past_esa.esa_runs[]
→ stats.past_esa.events[]
```

**Get approved incentives for a stream:**
```
GET /api/incentives?stream=stream1&status=Approved
```

**Poll a sync job until complete:**
```
POST /api/admin/sync/runners
→ { id: "...", status: "pending" }
GET /api/admin/jobs/{id}   (poll every 3s until status == "completed" or "failed")
```
